name: Mute Swan
summary: The mute swan is a large, elegant waterbird famous for its pure white feathers, graceful S-shaped neck, and bright orange bill. Living on lakes, ponds, and slow rivers across Europe and Asia, they are known for mating for life and protecting their nests fiercely.
metaTitle: Mute Swan Facts | Habitat, Diet, Behavior & Photos
metaDescription: Mute swan facts for kids with photos, freshwater habitat, diet, S-shaped neck, cygnet babies, and protective behavior on one easy-to-read page.
speed: Up to 50 mph (80 km/h) flight speed
behaviors: hissing and flapping wings to defend territory
adaptations: long flexible S-shaped neck to reach deep plants
waterproof feathers with oil from preen gland
strong wings for flight and defense
serrated edges on bill for cutting pondweed
funFacts: A baby swan is called a cygnet! Cygnets are born with fluffy grey or brown feathers and often ride on their parents' backs while swimming to stay warm and safe.
Although they are called 'mute' swans, they are not completely silent—they can make snorting, whistling, and loud hissing sounds when they are angry.
Mute swans are among the heaviest flying birds in the world, with males sometimes weighing up to 30 pounds (14 kg)!
A pair of swans usually mates for life. They build massive nests of reeds and grasses on the water's edge, which they use year after year.
When defending their nest or babies, swans will flare their wings, arch their necks, and hiss loudly. They can flap their strong wings to hit predators.
To feed, swans do not dive underwater; instead, they tilt their bodies forward (up-ending) and use their long necks to reach plants growing on the lake bed.

faq.question: What is a mute swan?
faq.answer: A mute swan is a very large, white waterbird belonging to the duck, goose, and swan family, famous for its graceful S-shaped neck and orange beak.

faq.question: Where do mute swans live?
faq.answer: They live on freshwater lakes, ponds, slow-moving rivers, and coastal lagoons in Europe, Asia, and North America.

faq.question: What do mute swans eat?
faq.answer: They are herbivores that eat mostly underwater plants, pondweed, algae, and grasses, using their long necks to reach plants deep below the surface.
